title: Equivalent Units — Weighted Average
event: Reyes Co.'s Mixing Department completed and transferred 40,000 units in June. Ending work in process is 10,000 units, 100% complete for materials and 40% complete for conversion. Compute equivalent units for materials and conversion (weighted average).

computationPaths.narration: Equivalent units translate partially finished physical units into whole-unit terms — separately for materials and conversion, because they enter production at different times.

memorize.body: WA equivalent units = units completed + (ending WIP × % complete). Compute materials and conversion SEPARATELY.
Materials often enter at the start (100% in ending WIP) while conversion accrues gradually — that's why the two EU figures differ.

questions.prompt: Completed 40,000; ending WIP 10,000 at 40% conversion. Equivalent units for CONVERSION = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the MATERIALS figure. Conversion is only 40% done in ending WIP: 40,000 + 4,000 = 44,000.
You ignored ending WIP entirely — its 40%-done units represent 4,000 equivalent units of work performed.
